title: Code Signing and Certificates
description: Understand and manage provisioning profiles, keystores, and certificates.

slides.title: Why Signing? 🔏
slides.content:
# Security and Identity

Code signing proves that the app comes from you and hasn't been tampered with. It's required for installing on real devices and publishing to stores.

- **iOS**: Uses Apple Developer certificates and provisioning profiles.
- **Android**: Uses a keystore (JKS) with a private key.

EAS Build can manage these for you automatically if you're part of the Apple Developer Program or have a keystore.

slides.title: iOS Signing with EAS
slides.content:
## Automatic Signing (Recommended)

EAS Build can create and manage your iOS certificates and profiles using its **credentials manager**. You just need to be enrolled in the Apple Developer Program ($99/year).

During the first build for iOS, EAS will guide you through the process, asking you to log in to your Apple account. It then creates and stores the necessary credentials.

Alternatively, you can provide your own certificates in the `credentials.json` file.

slides.title: Android Signing
slides.content:
For Android, you need a keystore file. EAS can generate one for you:

```bash
eas credentials
```

This command lets you manage credentials, including generating a new keystore. The keystore is securely stored on Expo's servers.

You can also provide your own keystore by placing it in the project and referencing it in `credentials.json`.

**Important**: Never lose your keystore! If you lose it, you cannot update your app on Google Play.

quizData.question: What is required to sign an iOS app for distribution?

options.text: An Apple Developer Program membership and provisioning profile
A code signing certificate from any CA

quizData.explanation: iOS requires an Apple Developer account, certificates, and provisioning profiles.

slides.title: ⚠️ Critical: Keep Your Keystore Safe
slides.content: For Android, if you lose your keystore, you cannot publish updates to your app – you'd have to create a new app with a new package name. Back it up securely!
